steps.title: Identify the hypothesis's prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis states: “interactions indicating high levels of cognitive engagement with ad content would result in relatively high levels of ad recall.” For a finding to directly support this, it must demonstrate that high-cognitive-engagement interactions actually produce higher ad recall than low-engagement interactions.

steps.title: Apply this to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice C directly compares commenting (high-engagement) with clicking links (lower-engagement) and shows commenters had significantly better ad recall two days later. The other choices measure different outcomes (A shows frequency, B measures purchasing behavior) or fail to compare engagement levels (D shows only high-engagement results without a low-engagement comparison). That matches choice C — C is correct.
